# Core AI Catalog ‚Äî Full LLM Context

## Identity

Core AI Catalog is a source-grounded registry of Apple Core AI (CoreML/MLX) models,
artifacts, benchmarks, provenance, and verified Apple AI terminology. It is an
independent community project ‚Äî not affiliated with or endorsed by Apple.

- **Repository:** https://github.com/kevinqz/coreai-catalog
- **License:** MIT
- **Version:** 1.7.0
- **Last verified:** 2026-07-01

## What this catalog IS

- A metadata and decision layer: discover, compare, verify, and install models
- Source-grounded: every record traces to Hugging Face, GitHub, or Apple docs
- Agent-first: CLI, MCP server, JSON exports, llms.txt ‚Äî all from the same engine

## What this catalog is NOT

- Not a model host ‚Äî it does not redistribute weights or artifacts
- Not legal advice ‚Äî `commercial_use` fields are triage labels, not permissions
- Not affiliated with Apple

## Catalog facts

- 79 model records (chat, vision-language, speech, embeddings, detection, more)
- 79 artifact provenance records (Hugging Face + GitHub sources)
- 66 benchmark records (throughput, latency, RTF ‚Äî iPhone + Mac)
- 42 verified Apple AI terminology entries (each citing an official source)
- 8 independent Hugging Face converters indexed (multi-upstream)
- Readiness scores (0-100) for deployment decision-making

## Data model

Six YAML files are the source of truth. All derived views (JSON, Markdown,
search indexes) are generated from them.

| File | Entity | Key |
|---|---|---|
| `catalog.yaml` | Model metadata | `models[].id` |
| `artifacts.yaml` | Artifact provenance | `artifacts[].id` |
| `benchmarks.yaml` | Benchmark measurements | `benchmarks[].model_id` |
| `sources.yaml` | Source registry | `sources[].id` |
| `upstreams.yaml` | Source taxonomy | group-based |
| `terms.yaml` | Apple AI terminology | `terms[].id` |

### Key relations

```
catalog.models[].artifact_ref  ‚Üí  artifacts.artifacts[].id
benchmarks.benchmarks[].model_id  ‚Üí  catalog.models[].id
catalog.models[].sources[]  ‚Üí  sources.sources[].id  OR  upstreams.*[].id
upstreams.original_model_sources[].applies_to[]  ‚Üí  catalog.models[].id
terms.terms[].relations[]  ‚Üí  terms.terms[].id
```

## How to consume this data

### CLI (humans)

```bash
pip install -e .
coreai-catalog search --capability vision-language --device iphone
coreai-catalog recommend --task "robot vision" --device iphone
coreai-catalog show qwen3-vl-2b
coreai-catalog compare qwen3-vl-2b gemma-4-e2b-vision
coreai-catalog install qwen3-vl-2b
coreai-catalog scores
coreai-catalog doctor
```

All CLI commands support `--json` for structured output.

### JSON exports (agents/automation)

All exports are committed to `dist/` and available via raw GitHub (no clone needed). Base URL: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kevinqz/coreai-catalog/main/dist/`

- `dist/catalog.json` ‚Äî all model records
- `dist/artifacts.json` ‚Äî all artifact provenance records
- `dist/benchmarks.json` ‚Äî all benchmark measurements
- `dist/sources.json` ‚Äî all source records
- `dist/upstreams.json` ‚Äî all upstream taxonomy
- `dist/terms.json` ‚Äî all terminology records
- `dist/search-index.json` ‚Äî flattened, denormalized model entries with joined data
- `dist/models.jsonl` ‚Äî one model per line (streaming-friendly)
- `dist/readiness-scores.json` ‚Äî 0-100 deployment readiness scores
- `dist/coreai-catalog.json` ‚Äî everything bundled

MCP server (for Claude Desktop, Cursor, MCP-compatible agents):

11 tools: `search_models`, `get_model`, `compare_models`, `recommend_model`,
`check_license`, `get_benchmarks`, `get_artifact`, `explain_term`,
`get_capabilities`, `get_tasks`, `get_version`.

All tools are read-only. Configuration:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "coreai-catalog": {
      "command": "python",
      "args": ["mcp_server/server.py"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Officiality model

Every artifact has an `officiality` block:

- `apple_export_recipe` ‚Äî true only for Apple official recipe conversions
- `apple_hosted_artifact` ‚Äî true only if Apple hosts the artifact (currently always false)
- `community_packaged` ‚Äî true for community-zoo packaged artifacts

Source groups:

- `official` ‚Äî Apple's own Core AI model zoo exports
- `zoo` ‚Äî Community-packaged from the Core AI model zoo
- `external` ‚Äî Independent HF converters not in the zoo

## Readiness score breakdown (0-100)

| Factor | Points |
|---|---|
| Artifact available | +15 |
| License commercial_use = likely | +10 |
| iPhone support | +10 |
| Mac support | +10 |
| Has benchmarks | +10 |
| Stock runtime | +10 |
| No custom kernel | +5 |
| No patch required | +5 |
| AOT supported | +5 |
| Status = confirmed | +10 |
| Confidence = high | +5 |
| Confidence = medium | +3 |
| Maturity = stable/active | +5 |
| Confidence = low | -10 |

Score is clamped to [0, 100].

## Capabilities in the catalog

chat, text-generation, vision-language, speech-to-text, text-to-speech,
embedding, reranking, object-detection, instance-segmentation,
promptable-segmentation, monocular-depth, image-generation, super-resolution,
document-ocr, audio-understanding, music-generation, text-to-audio,
text-to-video, gui-grounding, vision-language-action, robotics, reasoning,
agentic, image-to-3d, image-text-similarity, visual-document-retrieval,
moe, mla, speculative-decoding, instruction-following, diffusion-lm, hybrid-llm

## Verified Apple AI terminology (42 terms)

Organized by Apple ecosystem layer:

- **System surfaces:** App Intents, Spotlight, Siri
- **Developer frameworks:** Core AI, Core ML, MLX, Vision, Speech, Natural Language
- **Model providers:** Foundation Models
- **Artifact formats:** .aimodel, .mlmodel, .mlpackage
- **Developer tools:** Xcode, Instruments, Core AI Converter
- **AI primitives:** AOT compilation, quantization, ANE, Neural Engine
- **Provider protocols:** App Intents API, Foundation Models API

Each term cites an official Apple source URL.

## Data quality guarantees

1. **Schema validation** ‚Äî every record validates against its JSON Schema
2. **Cross-reference integrity** ‚Äî all artifact_ref, model_id, and source refs resolve
3. **Zero unknowns** ‚Äî critical fields use `not_published` if upstream doesn't disclose
4. **Officiality logic** ‚Äî source_group: official ‚Üí apple_export_recipe: true
5. **Append-only benchmarks** ‚Äî superseded values retained with confidence: needs_review

## Derived fields

Several fields are inferred from existing data rather than requiring upstream collection:

- **`architecture`** ‚Äî inferred from the `runner` field (e.g., CoreAIRunner ‚Üí transformer, CoreAIDiffusionPipeline ‚Üí diffusion, CoreAIImageSegmenter ‚Üí cnn/transformer, CoreAITranscribe ‚Üí transformer, CoreAIVideoPipeline ‚Üí diffusion, CoreAIKit-GraphModel ‚Üí encoder)
- **`context_window`** ‚Äî extracted from model `notes` where mentioned (e.g., "128K context")
- **`streaming`** ‚Äî inferred from model type: chat/text-generation/LLM models support streaming (`true`); detection, segmentation, embedding, and image models do not (`false`)
- **`size.artifact_size`** ‚Äî serves as a proxy for **minimum memory footprint**: the artifact must be fully loaded into memory at runtime, so `artifact_size` approximates the minimum RAM required to run the model on-device (actual peak memory may be higher due to KV cache, activation buffers, and framework overhead)
- **Benchmark `precision`** ‚Äî when the upstream benchmark source does not publish precision, it is inferred from the model's catalog precision and marked with an `inferred:` prefix
- **Benchmark `higher_is_better`** ‚Äî derived from the metric type: throughput/RTF metrics ‚Üí `true`, latency/footprint metrics ‚Üí `false`

## Canonical files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/llms.txt` | Concise LLM context map |
| `/llms-full.txt` | This file ‚Äî full context |
| `/agent.json` | Machine-readable agent manifest |
| `/AGENTS.md` | Instructions for AI agents |
| `/openapi.yaml` | REST API contract (mirrors MCP tools) |
| `/dist/coreai-catalog.json` | Complete bundled JSON export |

## Update policy

- YAML sources are updated as upstream changes are discovered
- Derived views (docs, JSON) are regenerated via `python scripts/generate.py`
- CI validates schemas, cross-references, and data quality on every push
- Weekly cron scans upstream (Hugging Face, GitHub) for new models/gaps
